query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "in-context world modeling for robotic control" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it directly investigates the mechanism of "world modeling" for robotic control and navigation, which is the core subject of the user's follow-up question on extending "In-Context World Modeling for Robotic Control." Although the specific technique (interactive replay vs. in-context learning) differs, the paper provides essential prior work on the same biological/algorithmic construct (world models) within the exact same domain (robotic control).
